title: Excavations at Tel Beth-Shean 1989-1996, Volume III: The 13th-11th Century BCE Strata in Areas N and S
author: Amihai Mazar and Nava Panitz-Cohen (eds.)
publisher: Israel Exploration Society / Hebrew University of Jerusalem
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted excavation report. Summaries in project's own words only.
summary: The excavation-report volume specifically covering the Late Bronze/Iron I transition strata (Areas N and S) at Tel Beth-Shean: the Egyptian garrison town's decline and the post-garrison Iron I town, including the twin-temple complex and the reused Egyptian monuments (Seti I stelae, a Ramesses II stela, and the Ramesses III statue) found in a 'Monuments Courtyard' fronting the temples. This is the excavation volume most directly relevant to the stratigraphic window the 1 Samuel 31 narrative is set in, distinct from Volume I (mazar-beth-shean-2006), which is Mazar's broader synthesis of the tell's full Late Bronze IIB-medieval sequence.
confidenceNotes: TO VERIFY against primary copy: identified via secondary bibliographic search (project/foundation publication records and academic citations of the volume), not direct page inspection. The stratum labels and find-context details used in claims.ts are drawn from Mazar's own popular-level synthesis of this material (mazar-2012-bar-saul-impaled) and secondary summaries describing this volume's contents, not from this project's own inspection of the volume itself. Exact page/plate citations remain unverified.
